name: Illinois Apple Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 450°F.
2. For the crust: In a mixing bowl, combine flour and salt. Cut in shortening until mixture is uniform. Add water and toss lightly with a fork until dough forms a ball. Divide dough into two parts.
3. On a lightly floured surface, roll out the bottom crust into a circle about 1/8-inch thick and about 1-1/2 inches larger than an inverted 9-inch pie plate. Gently ease dough into the pie plate without stretching it. Trim edge even with the pie plate.
4. For the apple filling: In a mixing bowl, combine all ingredients except butter. Stir until well mixed. Spoon into the unbaked pie shell.
5. Use the remaining half of dough to roll out the top crust. Lift onto the filled pie and trim 1/2-inch beyond the edge of the pie plate. Fold top edge under the bottom crust and flute as desired.
6. Cut slits or designs in the top crust to allow steam to escape. Brush with milk and sprinkle with white sugar.
7. Bake at 450°F for 20 minutes. Reduce heat to 350°F and bake an additional 25 to 30 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown. Cool and serve.
